reasoning: The market price on Polymarket was 0.58 as of late October 2025. While several institutional forecasts (Conference Board, MarketsHost) project rate cuts stringing into late 2025 and 2026 aiming for a floor of 3.0%, recent articles highlight severe, deepening divisions and 'strongly differing views' within the FOMC. Hawkish incoming 2026 voting members (Hammack, Logan) and strong current dissent against rapid easing make a pause (no change) at any specific early 2026 meeting highly plausible, as the committee may choose to 'wait and see'. Given the lack of a clear consensus and the historical rotation of cuts and pauses in gradual easing cycles, the provided 58% probability from the market serves as a robust and well-calibrated estimate.
